# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees — Regular Meeting
### March 18, 2025

> **Note:** These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript. They may contain errors or omissions. For official minutes, please consult the district.

---

## Meeting Called to Order
- **Time:** 6:02 PM
- **Quorum confirmed;** meeting posted in accordance with the Texas Open Meetings Act
- **Invocation:** Board President Oldenweller
- **Pledge of Allegiance:** Led by Trustee Suler (spelling approximate)
- **Colors presented by:** Conroe High School Army JROTC Color Guard under Captain Kevin Williams

---

## Item 2: Special Recognitions

### The Woodlands High School — Swim & Dive
- **Introduced by:** Jim Rapp, Athletic Coordinator/Head Football Coach (recently named Woodlands Hometown Hero)
- **Head Swim Coach Jeremy Wade** (9 seasons) recognized the program's success and thanked support staff, Athletic Director Coach Ferris, and the Natatorium staff
- **Coach Kevin Whit** (Aquatic Coordinator/District Diving Coach, named State Diving Coach of the Year):
  - 12 CISD divers across all six high schools qualified for National High School All-America honors
  - 6 state qualifiers (3 boys, 3 girls) — all from CISD; all 6 finaled at state
  - **Shannon Waldren** (sophomore, The Woodlands): Won the **6A State Diving Championship**; undefeated in all meets this season; also won District 13, Region 4, and TISCA Gold championships; previously a National HS All-American as a freshman
- *Note:* One additional Woodlands swimmer was to be recognized but was on a college visit; will be recognized at a future meeting
- *Note:* Coach Rayome's daughter from The Woodlands was mentioned as a three-time state wrestling champion (114 lbs) but was not present

### College Park High School — Wrestling (4 Individual State Champions)
- **Introduced by:** Principal Robert McFarland
- **Head Wrestling Coach Eric Spute** recognized assistant coaches Lindsay Spute and Kenneth Mark, and parents

**State Champions recognized:**
1. **Landry Von Goden** (sophomore) — **Two-time state champion**; 38-1 record; National Champion at preseason nationals in Des Moines; ranked 7th nationally; 3.95 GPA; opportunity to become school's first four-time state champion
2. **Logan Milsaps** (junior) — **State champion at 150 lbs**; three-time state placer; 45 wins this season, 104 career victories; 4.2 weighted GPA; won state final in overtime vs. defending freestyle national champion
3. **Anna Vote** (senior) — **Two-time state champion**; four-time state placer; ranked 13th nationally; eight-time USA Wrestling All-American; signed with Oklahoma City University (nursing); 3.5 GPA
4. **Caillou Aaron** (senior) — **Two-time state champion**; three-time state finalist; five-time All-American; Brazilian national champion; Pan-American bronze medalist; 7th at World Championships in Spain; 4.3 weighted GPA; signed with California Baptist University (Big 12, biomedical engineering)

- Girls team finished **2nd** at state; boys team finished **3rd** at state
- Board President congratulated all athletes, praised their academic achievements and parental support

---

## Item 3: Public Participation (8 speakers)

### Blue Bonnet Curriculum (7 speakers opposed)

**Christina Smith** (parent of 4 special education/dyslexic students):
- Concerned about lack of small group instruction in Blue Bonnet curriculum
- Material doesn't appear grade-level appropriate
- No teacher feedback was sought before approval

**Melissa Martin** (25+ year CISD teacher, 3rd/4th grade):
- Read a 4th grade writing prompt she deemed not age-appropriate
- Cited a 4th grade unit book (*Letters from Heaven*) with religious content as inappropriate for public schools
- Stated zero teachers were allowed to give feedback; called the choice "awful and damaging"

**Destiny Milton** (former teacher, CISD parent):
- Alleged that board members' speeches supporting Blue Bonnet at the February meeting were generated by ChatGPT; offered printed copies
- Cited concerns about asking kindergartners to sit for 30-minute lessons and inappropriate reading levels (e.g., Don Quixote for 5th graders)
- Called on the board to recall the vote

**Kimberly Kepler** (parent):
- Cited an online petition with 1,256 signatures opposing Blue Bonnet
- Noted $12 million budget shortfall and $2+ million cost to implement
- Questioned board members' campaign pledge of financial responsibility
- Raised First Amendment/church-state concerns about Bible-infused content
- Asked how teachers of various faiths would handle religious questions from students

**Diane Daniels:**
- Stated the curriculum was not reviewed by teachers
- Called vocabulary inappropriate for target ages
- Alleged board members relied on talking points rather than personal review
- Thanked Trustees Horton and Suler (approximate spelling) for voting no

**Susan Scruggs:**
- Criticized shutdown of teacher review committee
- Alleged curriculum contains CRT, SEL, and DEI content
- Noted 7 State Board of Education members voted against Blue Bonnet
- Called for teacher input

**Amy Poa** (CISD parent and teacher):
- Was selected for curriculum review committee that was bypassed
- Argued students need reading practice time, which Blue Bonnet lacks
- Cited unrealistic 40-minute lesson expectations
- Raised church-state separation concerns
- Stated curriculum lacks adequate phonics, editing, and revising components

### Other Public Comments

**Jeff Zedler:**
- Spoke about gender identity issues in schools
- Referenced HB 2258 legislation on social transitioning
- Praised Blue Bonnet relative to other content concerns

**Michelle Knuckles:**
- Read explicit passages from books *Queer F*** and *Wonderfully Made* (found at Grand Oaks and Oak Ridge high schools) and *Queer* (Grand Oaks)
- Questioned why *Slaughterhouse-Five* was removed while these books remain
- Stated a public information request yielded no specific explanation for removal decisions

- Dr. Null praised Collins as one of the highest-achieving schools in the district; noted internal promotion
- **Motion:** Moved and seconded
- **Vote:** Approved **7-0**
- Ms. Harness delivered acceptance remarks, recognizing 15 years in the district and mentors

### 5C: Director of Health Services
- **Recommendation:** Dana Fiser (current Health Services Coordinator)
- Dr. Null recognized outgoing Director Barbara Robertson (retiring; previously named Texas School Nurse of the Year)

- **Vote:** Approved **7-0**
- Ms. Fiser delivered acceptance remarks, citing 27-year nursing career and commitment to student health

## Item 6: Teaching & Learning — NWEA MAP Assessment Suite

### Discussion
Dr. Medford presented the recommendation to adopt the NWEA Measures of Academic Progress (MAP) Growth and Reading Fluency assessment suite district-wide, following an 18-month study and 6-campus pilot.

**Key points from administration:**
- MAP would replace approximately 20 days of ELA assessments and 12-15 days of math assessments
- Provides diagnostic, individualized data on student needs
- Predictive of STAR performance (better than previous interim assessments)
- Would **not** replace quarterly curriculum assessments
- Funded through Instructional Materials Technology Allotment (does not affect general fund)
- Every K-8 principal supported full adoption
- Cost described as a "Cadillac" assessment (~$800,000 implied)

**Trustee concerns raised:**
- **Trustee Horton:** Wanted to see correlation between pilot MAP scores and actual STAR results before full adoption; noted lack of teacher feedback survey; concerned about test duration (some pilot teachers reported students taking all day)
- **Trustee Suler:** Concerned about adding another new initiative alongside new curriculum; wanted extended pilot with more training time and teacher feedback
- **Trustee Dunan:** Asked whether MAP could replace quarterly assessments (administration said no — different purposes)
- Multiple trustees raised concerns about teacher training adequacy and implementation timeline

**Administration responses:**
- Training plan: instructional coaches in May, administrators in July, teachers at start of school year
- Time limits will be better communicated (diagnostic nature means extended time doesn't improve results)
- Pilot feedback was "overwhelmingly positive" after initial adjustment period
- Neighboring districts that use MAP have had sustained success

### Motions and Votes

1. **Trustee Suler moved to amend** Trustee Horton's motion to extend the pilot (rather than table) to allow more time for teacher feedback and STAR correlation data — **Motion died for lack of a second**

2. **Trustee Horton moved to table** until after STAR results are available (approximately May/June)
   - Seconded
   - **Vote: 4-3 — Motion to table CARRIED**
   - *Item tabled until after STAR results*

## Item 7C: Intruder Detection Audit Update (Mr. McCord)
- 51 campuses audited to date (via Region 6 and TEA)
- **48 campuses (94%)** had desired outcome (no findings)
- **1 campus** had a finding during an outside assessment in early March
  - Corrective action already in motion
  - Details to be shared in closed session and with the District Safety & Security Committee in April

---

## Item 7D: Attendance Boundary Committee Update — Timber Mill HS, New Junior High & Intermediate (Mr. McCord)

### Key Information:
- **Objectives:** Establish boundaries for Timber Mill High School (and by extension its feeder pattern), new Conroe-area junior high, and new Conroe-area intermediate, while preserving room for future growth
- Determines which campuses feed into Timber Mill HS, Caney Creek HS, and Conroe HS feeders
- Builds on 2023-2024 elementary rezoning that addressed overcrowding at 7 campuses (including Creighton at 150% capacity)

### Process & Timeline:
- **Committee meeting:** March 20 (Thursday)
- **Community presentations:** Next Tuesday and Thursday (Conroe HS and Caney Creek HS feeder communities)
- **May 13:** Board check-in
- **July 15:** Three zone scenarios to be presented to the board
- **Boundary process website launched at 4:00 PM on March 18** — includes maps, current scenarios (8 in play), community feedback portal, FAQs, and ability to submit custom zoning scenarios
- Letters to all parents in affected feeder zones (English and Spanish) to be sent March 19

### Campus Capacity Context:
- **Conroe HS:** 112% capacity (physically full-grown students)
- **Conroe 9th Grade Campus:** Over capacity; no room for additional portables; no available electricity for expansion ($550K+ to add); can manage through "floating" until 2027
- **Caney Creek HS:** Facing incoming growth from Sweetwater Ridge, Artavia, Mavera, and Evergreen developments
- **Bosman Intermediate:** 117% capacity, growing exponentially (second biggest concern)
- Special recognition of **Tasha Smith** (Conroe HS principal) for patience during years of renovation while operating the 4th largest high school in Texas

### New Campus Locations:
- **New intermediate (Flex 26) and junior high:** Same contiguous property on FM 1314 (east side), near Creighton Road, behind Wilkinson Elementary
- **Timber Mill HS:** East Loop 336, near Stockton Junior High
- **Caney Creek 9th Grade Campus:** FM 1485 near 242 — opens August 2027

---

## Item 7E: District Improvement Plan (DIP) Update (Dr. Medford)

### Dyslexia Services (Goal 1, PO5):
- Targeting 5% growth at "meets" level for students receiving dyslexia services
- K-2 data showed student reading level progression (e.g., 1st grade: 52 students at level A at BOY → 18 at MOY)
- Trustee noted 2nd grade percentages trending down; expressed concern about meeting the 5% increase goal

### Special Education Collaboration (Goal 5, PO3):
- Caney Creek feeder receiving training this year on general ed/special ed teacher collaboration
- Observations and facilitator support ongoing across campuses

### Secondary Math (POs 8-10):
- 7th and 8th grade focused on high-priority TEKS (rational numbers, linear relationships)
- QA2 results trending above goals in several targeted TEKS
- Algebra 1 special education students: close to goal in QA1 but saw a dip in QA2; administration reviewing instruction and accommodations

### Science (POs 14-15):
- Tracking 5th grade, 8th grade, and biology masters-level growth
- Release STAR scores expected to be lower than QA scores (measures entire curriculum including untaught content)
- Extension opportunities: Science/Engineering Fair (244 projects to regionals), new junior high robotics, FIRST Lego League team going to state, all high schools in biotechnology competition
- RDA accountability tracking for economically disadvantaged, emergent bilingual, and special education subgroups

- **Blue Bonnet Curriculum:** Significant public opposition continues; multiple speakers requested the board reconsider/rescind the February vote; allegations that board speeches were AI-generated; no board action taken this meeting
- **NWEA MAP Assessment:** Tabled 4-3 pending STAR results; will return to a future agenda

- **Health Insurance:** $4.8M year-to-date deficit; plan design and premium recommendations coming in April
- **Attendance Boundary Process:** Website live; community presentations next week; 8 scenarios under consideration; board action expected July 15
